author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
gurau śrī⋅gaurāṅge tad⋅udita⋅su⋅bhakti⋅prakaraṇe
śacī⋅sunor⋅līlā⋅vikasita⋅su⋅tīrthe nija⋅manau
harer namni preṣṭhe hari⋅tithiṣu rupānuga⋅jane
śuka⋅prokte śāstre prati⋅jani mamāstāṁ khalu ratiḥ

sadā vṛndāranye madhura⋅rasa⋅dhanye rasa⋅mayaḥ
parāṁ śaktiṁ rādhāṁ parama⋅rasa⋅mūrtiṁ ramayati
sa caivāyam kṛṣṇo nija⋅bhajana⋅mudrām⋅upadiśan
śacī⋅sūnur gauḍe prati⋅jani mamāstāṁ prabhu⋅varaḥ

na vairāgyaṁ grāhyaṁ bhavati na hi jad bhakti⋅janitaṁ
tathā jñānaṁ bhānaṁ citi jadi viśeṣaṁ na manute
spṛhā me nāṣṭaṅge hari⋅bhajana⋅saukhyaṁ na hi jatas
tato rādhā⋅kṛṣṇa⋅pracura⋅paricaryā bhavatu me

kuṭīre ‘pi kṣudre vraja⋅bhajana⋅yogye taru⋅tale
śacī⋅sūnos tīrthe bhavatu nitarāṁ me nivasatiḥ
na cānyatra kṣetre vibudha⋅gaṇa⋅sevye pulakito
vasāmi prāsāde vipula⋅dhana⋅rājyānvita iha

na varṇe saktir me na khalu mamatā hyāśrama⋅vidhau⋅
na dharme nddharme mama ratir ihāste kvacid api
param tat⋅tad⋅dharme mama jaḍa⋅śarīram dhṛtam idam
ato dharmān sarvān su⋅bhajana⋅sahāyānn abhilaṣe

su⋅dainyaṁ sāralyaṁ sakala⋅sahanaṁ mānada⋅danaṁ
dayāṁ svīkṛtya śrī⋅hari⋅carana⋅sevā mama tapaḥ
sadācāro ‘sau me prabhu⋅pada⋅parair jaḥ samuditaḥ
prabhos caitanyasyākṣaya⋅carita⋅pīyūṣa⋅kṛtiṣu

na vaikuṇṭhe rājye na ca viṣaya⋅kārye mama ratir
na nirvāṇe mokṣe mama matir ihāste kṣaṇam api
vrajānandād anyad hari⋅vilasitaṁ pāvanam api
kathañcin māṁ rādhānvaya⋅virahitaṁ no sukhayati

na me patnī⋅kanyā⋅tanaya⋅jananī⋅bandhu⋅nicayā
harau bhakte bhaktau na khalu jadi teṣāṁ su⋅mamatā
abhaktānām⋅anna⋅grahaṇam api dośo viṣayiṇāṁ
kathaṁ teṣāṁ saṅgād hari⋅bhajana⋅siddhir bhavati me?

asat⋅tarkair⋅andhān jaḍa⋅sukha⋅parān kṛṣṇa⋅vimukhān
ku⋅nirvāṇāsaktān satatam ati⋅dūre pariharan
arādhāṁ govindaṁ bhajati nitarāṁ dāmbhikatayā
tad⋅abhyāse kintu kṣaṇam api na jāmi vratam idam

prasādānna⋅kṣīrāśana⋅vasana⋅pātrādibhir ahaṁ
padārthair nirvāhya vyavahṛtim asaṅgaḥ ku⋅viṣaye
vasann īśa⋅kṣetre jugala⋅bhajanānandita⋅manas
tanuṁ mokṣye kāle juga⋅pada⋅parāṇāṁ pada⋅tale

śacī⋅sūnor⋅ājñā⋅grahaṇa⋅caturo jo vraja⋅vane
parārādhyāṁ rādhām bhajati nitarām kṛṣṇa⋅rasikām
ahaṁ tvetat⋅pādāmṛtam anudinaṁ naiṣṭhika⋅manā
vaheyaṁ vai pītvā śirasi ca mudā sannati⋅yutaḥ

harer dāsyaṁ dharmo mama tu cira⋅kālaṁ prakṛtito
mahā⋅māyā⋅yogād⋅abhinipatitaḥ duḥkha⋅jaladhau
ito jāsyāmy ūrdhvaṁ sva⋅niyama⋅su⋅ratya prati⋅dinaṁ
sahāyo me mātraṁ vitatha⋅dalanī vaiṣṇava⋅krpā ⋅

kṛtaṁ kenāpyetat sva⋅bhajana⋅vidhau svaṁ niyamakāṁ
paṭhed jo viśraddhaḥ priya⋅yugala⋅rūpe ‘rpita manaḥ
vraje rādhā⋅kṛṣṇau bhajati kila saṁprāpya nilayaṁ
sva⋅mañjaryāḥ paścād vividha⋅varivasyāṁ sa kurute
en_translation:
May my attachment, in every birth, be to the Spiritual Master, to Lord Gaurāṅga, to the practices of pure devotion taught by Him, to the holy places where the Son of Mother Śacī performed His pastimes, to my own mantra, to the Holy Name of Lord Hari, to the holy days of the Lord, to the followers of Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī, and to the scriptures spoken by Śukadeva Gosvāmī.

May that same Lord Kṛṣṇa, who always enjoys in the forest of Vṛndāvana with Rādhā—the supreme potency and personification of the highest mellows—and who has appeared as the Son of Mother Śacī in the land of Gauḍa teaching His own method of worship, be my supreme Lord in every birth.

Renunciation that is not born of devotion is not acceptable. Similarly, knowledge and realization are of no special value if they do not lead to spiritual consciousness. I have no desire for the eight-fold yoga process, because there is no happiness of worshipping Lord Hari within it. Therefore, may I be blessed with abundant service to Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa.

May my residence always be in the holy land of the Son of Mother Śacī, even if it is in a tiny cottage suitable for the worship of Vraja, or under a tree. I have no desire to reside even in a palace endowed with vast wealth and a kingdom in any other place, even if it were served by the demigods.

I have no attachment to social class or the regulations of the spiritual orders of life. My attachment is not at all in pious or impious deeds. But since this material body is sustained by those duties, I desire all such duties only as they serve as aids to pure devotional service.

Great humility, simplicity, tolerance of all things, giving respect to others, and accepting mercy—the service of the lotus feet of Lord Hari is my only austerity. This good conduct of mine has been spoken of by those devoted to the lotus feet of the Lord in their descriptions of the nectarean character of Lord Caitanya.

I have no attachment to the spiritual world of Vaikuṇṭha, nor to worldly activities. My mind is not interested even for a moment in impersonal liberation. Other than the bliss of Vraja, even the pure pastimes of Lord Hari do not make me happy if they are without a connection to Rādhā.

I do not care for my wife, children, mother, or any relatives if they do not have deep affection for Lord Hari, His devotees, and the path of devotion. Even accepting food from non-devotees and materialistic people is a fault. How then, through their association, could I ever attain perfection in the worship of Lord Hari?

I will always keep far away from those who are blinded by false arguments, those devoted to material happiness, those averse to Lord Kṛṣṇa, and those who worship Lord Govinda without Rādhā out of great pride. I will not follow their practices for even a single moment; this is my vow.

Managing my life with the food, milk, clothing, and utensils produced in the holy land, and remaining unattached to worldly affairs, I will reside in the land of the Lord. With a mind delighted by the worship of the Divine Couple, I will give up my body at the lotus feet of those who are devoted to Them.

With a firm mind, I will daily carry upon my head and drink the nectar of the lotus feet of that person who is expert in following the orders of the Son of Mother Śacī, and who worships with great devotion the supremely worshipable Rādhā in the forests of Vraja.

Servitude to Lord Hari is my natural and eternal duty. I have fallen into the ocean of misery due to the influence of the great illusory energy. From this state I will rise daily with my own regulated practice and great attachment. My only helper is the mercy of the devotees, which destroys all illusion.

Whoever reads this hymn, which was composed as a set of regulations for the method of worship, with great faith and a mind offered to the Divine Couple, will surely attain Their abode and worship Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa in Vraja, performing various services as a maidservant.
